title: sm-max-tokens-truncation
description:
This is a continuation of sm-max-tokens-truncation. In this interval, the same SM failure mode appeared again: an `sm` run failed at 2026-05-28T11:23:28.580471+00:00 with explicit truncation evidence in the error text, `json parse failed at max_tokens=65536 finish_reason=length`. The error is self-diagnosing: model output was cut off at the token cap and the truncated payload then failed JSON parsing.

The immediate impact in this window is small because the failed SM call was followed by another successful SM run and then a successful reviewer run, with no queue-skew, tick-duration, or spend anomaly observed in the same window. However, this failure class has already been recorded in multiple prior concerns, including prior `warn` and `halt` assessments, so the current event extends an already-established recurring pattern rather than introducing a one-off anomaly.

The surrounding burst of sandbox-style failures on onboarder, test_implementer, and dev is not strongly connected to this concern: those events are story-less, zero-cost, and mostly report ambiguous `tests not green after run` errors from pytest temp worktrees. The coherent signal here is specifically the repeated SM truncation-at-max-tokens behavior. Given the prior history and the explicit error text, the most plausible area remains SM model/prompt/runtime settings rather than story-domain logic.

Because the same clearly diagnosable factory-side failure class has now recurred across multiple intervals, this concern remains severe even though the single event in this window self-recovered. L3 judgment is needed to determine why SM is still occasionally hitting the 65536-token ceiling and emitting incomplete JSON despite prior recurrence.

evidence.summary_excerpt: This window included a burst of sandbox/test-context run failures plus one successful and one failed sm run. The sm failure is diagnosable from the error text: model output hit max_tokens and was truncated during JSON parsing.

evidence.excerpt: persona=sm success=false error="json parse failed at max_tokens=65536 finish_reason=length: Unterminated string starting at: line 1 column 9 (char 8)" model=deepseek/deepseek-chat tokens_in=40 tokens_out=80
persona=sm success=true model=deepseek/deepseek-chat attempt_n=754 tokens_in=20 tokens_out=40
persona=sm success=true model=deepseek/deepseek-chat attempt_n=756 tokens_in=10 tokens_out=20

{"noteworthy":"Six failed runs appeared; five are ambiguous sandbox-style test failures, but one sm failure is clearly diagnosable from its own error text as max_tokens/output truncation during JSON parsing."}

escalation_reason: Repeated SM max-tokens truncation has persisted across multiple watcher-note intervals and prior L2 concerns, including earlier halt-level concerns. The pattern is diagnostically clear from the explicit `max_tokens=65536 finish_reason=length` error text, and L3 judgment is needed because the same factory-attributable failure class continues to recur despite prior detection.
